description:
'''Lekompo''' is a South African music genre that emerged in [[Limpopo]] during the early 2020s. The genre incorporates elements of Bolobedu house, tsa manyalo, Shangaan electro, gqom, and amapiano, and is characterized by fast-paced rhythms, prominent synthesizers, and lyrics reflecting the experiences of working-class communities in Limpopo and Mpumalanga.

The genre gained national prominence through artists such as [[Kharishma]], [[Makhadzi]], Shebeshxt, Shandesh and Tribby Wadi Bhozza. By the mid-2020s, Lekompo had become one of South Africa's fastest-growing music genres and attracted attention from music industry organizations and streaming platforms.
